decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). Following the strategy shift suggestion, I will now coordinate a geometric expansion of the heat spreader. Increasing both width and height will enlarge the thermal cross-section and lateral spreading area, reducing thermal resistance without violating material bounds.

changes.reason: Widening the spreader to the domain limit (1.0m) maximizes lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.
Increasing spreader height by 40% (within 1.5x limit) adds vertical thermal mass and improves conduction path to the base plate.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the base plate
Lower overall thermal resistance from chip to sinks
risk_notes: Spreader width is now at the maximum domain limit; future iterations cannot widen further.
Increased spreader height reduces clearance to the top boundary; verify no overlap issues in future vertical moves.
Geometric changes may slightly alter mesh quality if not adapted, though current mesh density should suffice.
